message:
refactor: extract shared IsVMRunning utility to pkg/vmutil

Both gc.go and resources.go had identical isVMRunning() implementations
that check if a Firecracker process is running via signal 0. This
consolidates them into a shared vmutil.IsVMRunning() function to
eliminate duplication and reduce risk of bugs when one copy is
updated without the other.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+// Package vmutil provides shared utilities for VM management.
+package vmutil
+
+import (
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strconv"
+	"strings"
+	"syscall"
+)
+
+// IsVMRunning checks if a VM in the given directory is running by checking
+// if the process in firecracker.pid exists and is signalable.
+func IsVMRunning(vmDir string) bool {
+	pidFile := filepath.Join(vmDir, "firecracker.pid")
+	data, err := os.ReadFile(pidFile)
+	if err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+
+	pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)))
+	if err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+
+	// Check if process exists by sending signal 0
+	err = syscall.Kill(pid, 0)
+	return err == nil
+}
